channel.title: My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Third Blog
item.description:
Unit 3

So, In Third unit we learn about Failure, we all know about when we are doing anything new we faces problems sometime we fail doing that thing so this is not about you fail in that works in this unit we learn what is failure and its consequences. When we are doing programming or coding data representation we face many errors and technical problems also, it feels bad sometimes. In this unit we read a case study and a chapter  so basically this whole work is a group work but we sit together and do the whole work very politely. So our case study is all about that Y2K bug. This is also known as The Millenium Bug or 2000s’ Bug. In this case study we found that in previous times computers used only the last two digits of the year to show the date. Like computer use 99 instead of 1999. As soon as the time came closer to the 90s. People would worry about what would happen in the midnight of 31st of december 1999. Newspaper printing news like that we see is any big scene will be created on 1 jan. Will the world end? This thing is very interesting that the rumours spread about what will happen at midnight? Did the computer stop working? Also people really face problems like if a baby was born in 1990 so sometimes computers put her D.O.B is 1880. Some people face problems in banking accounts and whole market banks, flights and government services get affected by this problem. But the good thing is that some people think this was in a positive way and they come together and make a plan on how to overcome this problem.Companies from the U.S.A spent hundred billion dollars to overcome this problem.
Another part of this assignment was summarising the chapter from book Right Kind of Wrong: The Science of Failing Well by Amy Edmondson. This chapter was really very interesting as from its name only (Context and Consequences) we can know that it will talk about contexts and their respective consequences. This was what my first impression was of this chapter but it was far more engaging than this (at least i found it interesting). It also discusses what kind of approach we should take to encounter these different types of failures and in what sense we can learn the best from them.

item.title: Second Blog
item.description: Unit 2  In this unit, I explore new and many interesting things related to digital images. At first I felt very confused but slowly I started understanding them better.  One of the important things I learned is how images are represented using bits. Colours like black and white images use very simple representations, where each pixel is either 1 or 0. But this is not same for all like coloured images are more complex because they use more bits to represent different colours. I found it interesting how a simple image is made with so much data. After that I learned about pixels, resolution,and how image quality depends on these factors. Before this unit I had no idea how these images were formed or stored. But now I am able to understand that every image we see on screen is actually a combination of many pixels.Another important topic was  colour representation. We discuss all about how coloured are stored and how editing tools can change brightness, contrast and other properties. This is very useful for me to understand how photo editing works in real life. We also have some practical work for these topics. These practicals made learning more interesting. At first,I found it a bit difficult to follow the steps, especially when using new tools and commands. But when I tried many times or did practice I became more comfortable. Doing hands-on work helped me understand the concepts better. Overall, this unit was very useful and engaging. It helped me see technology from a different perspective. Now with the help of this unit I can understand how digital images work, not just use them.I believe this knowledge will help me for future studies and projects.

item.title: My First Post
item.description: Unit 1 reflection blog In my first class, Becoming a reflective scientist course helped me with how decisions are made in organizations. We also see how companies and governments are releasing new policies , updates and rules. These decisions take affirmative actions, they are not arbitrary or poorly justified. Instead they must be based on existing data.The moment the instructor gave us a task, finding how data is organised into  a smart phone, I was so excited. I have started thinking from the perspective of a business person, somewhat changing my perspective. This task has given me that feeling, sometimes I really lost myself into it. Thereafter I was curious about the next class. Usually we have regular classes, where teaching and discussion happens and lab classes where some skills are taught. The next class was a lab. We were divided into groups. I was so terrified when I saw the complex mathematical equation on lab assignments. My mind was blank. Then, upon  following the instructions of the instructor ,I could do it. Surprise, what ? Those equations were not intended to solve , rather we  have to print them in document exactly how they are, but this time  using zettler. The first time I  understood how  textbooks are designed. Really it is a beauty. After going back to the room , I used chatGTP to explore it in further possible ways.
